title: आरती कुंज बिहारी की
devanagariText:
आरती कुंजबिहारी की,
श्री गिरिधर कृष्ण मुरारी की॥

गले में बैजन्ती माला,
बजावै मुरली मधुर बाला।
श्रवन में कुण्डल झलकाला,
नन्द के आनन्द नन्दलाला,
गोपी मन चुरानेवाला॥
आरती कुंजबिहारी की...॥

घाट कदम्ब के तट पर,
भोजन करें नन्द के घर।
मैया यशोदा बल-बल जाये,
नन्दलाला दूध पी के सो जाये,
माखन चुराने जाये॥
आरती कुंजबिहारी की...॥

कनक समान देह दमके,
नयनन में कजरा चमके।
मोर मुकुट शिर ऊपर राजे,
मोहन बंसी बजी साजे,
दुनिया रची जिन आजे॥
आरती कुंजबिहारी की...॥

चरण कमल पर राजत बाजे,
नेवरी बजत सुहावन लाजे।
कटि पट पीत विराजत राजे,
कुंजबिहारी गिरिधर लाजे,
भक्तन की लाज बचावे॥
आरती कुंजबिहारी की...॥

जहाँ ते प्रकट भई गंगा,
सकल मनोरथ पूरण करें।
जय श्री गिरिधर कुंजबिहारी,
जय श्री कुंजबिहारी,
श्री गिरिधर कृष्ण मुरारी की॥
आरती कुंजबिहारी की...॥
transliteration:
Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki,
Shri Giridhar Krishna Murari Ki.

Gale Mein Baijanti Mala,
Bajavai Murali Madhur Bala.
Shravan Mein Kundal Jhalkala,
Nand Ke Anand Nandlala,
Gopi Man Churanewala.
Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki...

Ghat Kadamb Ke Tat Par,
Bhojan Karein Nand Ke Ghar.
Maiya Yashoda Bal-Bal Jaye,
Nandlala Doodh Pi Ke So Jaye,
Makhan Churane Jaye.
Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki...

Kanak Saman Deh Damke,
Naynan Mein Kajra Chamke.
Mor Mukut Shir Upar Raje,
Mohan Bansi Baji Saje,
Duniya Rachi Jin Aaje.
Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki...

Charan Kamal Par Rajat Baje,
Nevri Bajat Suhavan Laje.
Kati Pat Peet Virajat Raje,
Kunj Bihari Giridhar Laje,
Bhaktan Ki Laj Bachave.
Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki...

Jahan Te Prakat Bhai Ganga,
Sakal Manorath Puran Karein.
Jai Shri Giridhar Kunj Bihari,
Jai Shri Kunj Bihari,
Shri Giridhar Krishna Murari Ki.
Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki...
hindiMeaning:
कुंजों में विहार करने वाले, गिरिधर श्रीकृष्ण मुरारी की आरती।

जिनके गले में वैजयन्ती माला शोभा पा रही है, जो मधुर बांसुरी बजाते हैं, जिनके कानों में कुंडल झलक रहे हैं, जो नंद बाबा के आनंद स्वरूप नन्दलाल हैं, जो गोपियों का मन चुराने वाले हैं।

कदम्ब के घाट और तट पर विराजते हैं, नंद के घर भोजन करते हैं। माता यशोदा उन पर बलिहारी जाती हैं, नन्दलाल दूध पीकर सो जाते हैं और माखन चुराने जाते हैं।

जिनका शरीर सोने के समान दमक रहा है, नयनों में काजल चमक रहा है, सिर पर मोर मुकुट सुशोभित है, मोहन की बाँसुरी सज कर बज रही है, जिन्होंने यह सारी दुनिया रची है।
benefits: Essential evening and morning prayer for Krishna devotees
Traditionally invoked to establish a devotional atmosphere in the home
Invokes the blessings of kunj bihari krishna
Traditionally recited for removal of negativity and brings peace to the household
Strengthens devotion and love for the Lord
description: Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki (आरती कुंज बिहारी की) is a traditional worship song (aarti) dedicated to Lord Krishna. Drawn from Traditional, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to strengthen devotion and love for the Lord, grant the blessings of Kunj Bihari Krishna, and remove negativity and bring peace to the household. It is especially recommended during Janmashtami and Holi for maximum spiritual benefit. At 5 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki in Hindi with English transliteration. The most popular Krishna aarti sung daily in temples across India.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Krishna Aarti 'Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki'?
seoFaqs.answer: The Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki is traditionally attributed to Sayyed Ibrahim 'Ras Khan', the celebrated 16th-century Krishna devotee whose verses are part of the wider Vrindavan bhakti tradition. The aarti has been carried forward orally through Pushti and Vaishnava lineages and is now popularly sung across India in T-Series and Hariharan recordings.

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Kunj Bihari' mean in the Krishna Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Kunj Bihari' literally means 'the one who roams in the kunj' — the flowering bowers and groves of Vrindavan where Krishna played his raas-leela with the gopis. The name evokes Krishna's playful, pastoral form rather than his royal Dwarka aspect, making this aarti especially loved by Vrindavan-tradition devotees and Pushtimarg followers.

seoFaqs.question: When should Krishna Aarti be performed?
seoFaqs.answer: Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki is sung daily during Sandhya aarti at Krishna temples, especially at sunset. It is considered most auspicious on Wednesdays (Krishna's day in some traditions), Ekadashi, Janmashtami, Radhashtami, the entire month of Kartik, and during Govardhan Puja. Many households also sing it after the morning Mangala aarti.

seoFaqs.question: What images of Krishna does the aarti describe?
seoFaqs.answer: The verses describe Krishna with a forest-flower garland (vanmala), peacock feather crown (mor mukut), kundal earrings, the murli (flute) at his lips, Tribhanga pose, and Radha by his side. Each image is a meditation cue — the singer is meant to visualise Kunj Bihari standing in Vrindavan's groves while performing the lamp circumambulation.

seoFaqs.question: How is Krishna Aarti different from a bhajan or kirtan?
seoFaqs.answer: An aarti is a short ritual hymn (5-7 verses) sung while waving a lit ghee lamp before the deity, marking the close of puja. A bhajan is a longer devotional song without the lamp ritual, and a kirtan is congregational call-and-response chanting. Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki is specifically the lamp-offering hymn, traditionally followed by the offering of bhog and pushpanjali.
